Cell.Data: Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

A migration template (Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file) consists of different sheets which are visible at the bottom of the migration template. You use the different sheets to specify the data that belongs to different data structures. For example the migration template for the migration object 'Product', contains a sheet for basic data, a sheet for plant data, and so on. Some sheets are mandatory, and some are optional.

A migration template is based on the active view of the relevant migration object. You can find information about the active view in the Microsoft Excel XML file. In the file, navigate to File </>-> Info</>. You can find the active view name under Properties </>-> Tags</>.

In the Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file, navigate to 'File' -> 'Options' -> 'Advanced'. Under the option 'When calculating this workbook:', ensure that the option 'Set precision as displayed' is selected.

The size limit for each file is 100 MB. You can upload multiple XML files at once by using a zip file. Note that the combined size of all the XML files you want to add to the zip file must not exceed 160MB. The maximum permitted size of the zip file is 100MB.
The 'Field List' sheet is one of the first sheets in the migration template. You use this sheet to get an overview of the expected data in one central location.
It contains information about the mandatory and optional sheets, as well as detailed information for each sheet (for example the expected data type and length for the fields in each sheet).
On the 'Field List' sheet, you can view the following information for each field in the migration template:
The name of the sheet, and whether it is mandatory or optional. Only mandatory sheets have the suffix 'Mandatory', for example 'Basic Data (Mandatory)'. All other sheets are optional.

You can quickly get an overview of the mandatory and optional sheets by looking at the color of the sheet names at the bottom of the migration template. The name of the mandatory sheets have the color orange, while the optional sheets have the color blue.
The group name for the fields in a sheet.
The individual fields in each sheet, as well as whether fields are mandatory for a sheet.
Information about the expected format of the individual fields, for example the data type and length.

You can quickly get an overview of the mandatory and optional fields by looking at the background color of the cells in each sheet. The cells of the mandatory fields have an orange background color, while cells of optional fields have no background color.

In the field list sheet, certain technical information about the fields is hidden by default. The columns 'SAP Structure' and 'SAP Field' (columns 8 and 9) are hidden by default. The column 'SAP Structure' is the technical name of the structure that the field belongs to. The column 'SAP Field' is the technical name of the field. To unhide these columns, select the columns adjacent to either side of the columns that you want to unhide. Right-click your selection, and choose 'Unhide'.

If you want to extract data from SAP ERP, the technical name of the structure and the technical name of the field often corresponds to the SAP ERP table name and field name. Also, the SAP Release Note (2568909) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud data migration content uses the technical names of the structures and fields.
For each migration template, you need to specify data for the mandatory sheets, and for the optional sheets that are relevant for your project:
Mandatory sheets represent the minimum set of data you must provide for data migration. Fill in all mandatory fields.
Use optional sheets depending on your migration scope and available legacy data.
In each sheet, the cells of the mandatory fields have an orange background color, while cells of optional fields have no background color.
Viewing Additional Information for Each Column

In row 8, you can view the field names in SAP S/4HANA, as well as additional information such as the expected format (for example the data type and length). Note that you must expand the row to view this additional information.
Some fields are mandatory, and some are optional. The wildcard character (‘*’) beside the name of a field indicates that the field is mandatory.
Although an optional sheet may contain mandatory columns, if the sheet is not relevant for your project, there is no need to fill the mandatory columns in the sheet with data.

Rows 4, 5, and 6 are hidden by default. Row 4 is the technical name of the structure (corresponds to the sheet name). Row 5 is the technical name of the field (corresponds to row 8 - the field description). Row 6 contains technical information such as the data type and length.
To unhide these rows, select the rows adjacent to either side of the rows that you want to unhide. Right-click your selection, and choose 'Unhide'.
You can view the data type for a field in row 8 (see 'Viewing Additional Information for Each Column' above). Depending on the field, one of the following data types may be required:

Letters, numbers, and special characters are permitted. In the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, you can map the values of certain fields with the data type text (usually those fields with Length: 80) to their correct SAP S/4HANA target values. You can do this value mapping in the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit when you start the transfer (in the step 'Convert Values').

Enter numbers with decimals in the relevant country-specific format, for example 12.34 (United States) or 12,34 (Germany). For fields with decimals, the declared length includes decimals (if required), for example if the information for the column states: Length: 8, Decimals: 3, then a number such as 12345.678 is permitted. Note that decimal places are not mandatory. In this example, you can specify a whole number up to length 8 without decimal places, for example ‘1’. This number would be set to ‘1.000’ internally. For negative numbers, ensure that a minus sign ('-') directly precedes the number, for example '-100'. Note that currencies with more than 3 decimal places are not supported.
Note that the maximum field length supported by Microsoft Excel is 15 digits (including decimals). If you have longer numbers, use the option for transferring data to S/4HANA using staging tables.

Enter the date in your country-specific format, for example 12/31/1998 (United States) or 31.12.1998 (Germany). Note that Microsoft Excel automatically recognizes different date formats and transforms them automatically to the correct XML format.
Enter the time in the format HH:MM:SS, for example 02:52:40

When copying data to a sheet from Microsoft Excel, always right-click the relevant cell and choose the paste option 'Values (V)'. Avoid pasting data that includes formatting and formulas into the migration template, as this will corrupt the structure of the XML migration template.
Using the Find and Replace Function

Do not use the Microsoft Excel function 'Find and Replace'. If you change data by using this function, you may also unintentionally change the field names and corrupt the structure of the XML migration template.
Ensure that you only save the migration template as a Microsoft Excel XML Spreadsheet 2003 file. Other file types are not supported by the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.
Do not make any changes to the structure of the migration template, specifically:
Do not delete, rename or change the order of any sheet in the migration template.
Do not change the formatting of any cells.
Do not hide, remove, or change the order of any of the columns in the migration template.
Any changes to the sheets will result in a corrupted XML structure. Such modified migration templates are not supported by the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.

Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Type*
Key that assigns the product to a group of products such as raw product, finished product or trading goods.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Group
Key that you use to group together several products or services with the same attributes, and to assign them to a particular product group.
Type: Text Length: 80

Description*
Text that describes the product in more detail. Note: You can maintain additional descriptions (for languages other than those provided on the 'Basic Data' sheet).
Type: Text Length: 40

Language Key*
Please provide the language for the product description.
Type: Text Length: 80

Base Unit of Measure (ISO Format)*
Unit of measure in which stocks of the product are managed. The system converts all the quantities you enter in other units of measure (alternative units of measure) to the base unit of measure. Note: define your alternative units of measure in sheet 'Alternative Unit of Measure'.
Type: Text Length: 80

Change Number
Number used to uniquely identify a change master record.
Type: Text Length: 80

Revision Level
Indicates a product or document's change status in addition to the change number.
Type: Text Length: 80

GTIN
The GTIN on the Basic Data sheet always relates to the base unit of measure. Note: if you have additional GTIN's for this unit please enter them on the Additional GTIN sheet. The GTIN entered here is always the Main GTIN. With internal number assignment, you enter the GTIN category but not a GTIN.
Type: Text Length: 18

GTIN Category
The EAN category defines the following attributes of the corresponding EAN: Type/method of number assignment (internal and/or external number assignment) and details as to whether alphanumerical EANs are allowed Check-digit algorithm that checks whether a check digit is required Prefix (for perishables EANs) Length of the EAN.
Type: Text Length: 80

Division
A way of grouping products, or services. The system uses divisions to determine the sales areas and the business areas for a product, or service. Please maintain the division for all sales relevant products.
Type: Text Length: 80

Old Product Number
Number under which you have managed the product so far or still manage it, for example, in another system or in a card index. The maximum length of field Old Product Number is identical with the defined length for product numbers in your system.
Type: Text Length: 40

Indicator: Batch Management Required
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Specifies whether the product is managed in batches ('X') or not (<blank>). Note: to assign the batch class to your product please use migration object 'Material classification'.
Type: Text Length: 1

Valid From
Date indicating until when the product is valid. This date must not be later than the beginning of the listing periods.
Type: Date

Competitor
Customer number of the competitor. The competitor number in the product master record serves to identify a competitive product as that of a particular competitor.
Type: Text Length: 80

Industry Standard Description
Description of the product in accordance with the appropriate industry standard (such as ANSI or ISO). This field is purely for information.
Type: Text Length: 18

Size/Dimensions
Text field that you can use as you like, for example, to record the size or dimensions of the product. The data you enter is merely for information and is not used by the system.
Type: Text Length: 32

Laboratory / Design Office
Key for the design office, laboratory, or laboratory worker responsible.
Type: Text Length: 80

External Product Group
Key that you can use to assign the product to an external product group or to a product group determined according to external systematics.
Type: Text Length: 80

General Item Category Group
Products grouping that helps the system to determine item categories during sales document processing.
Type: Text Length: 80

Authorization Group
The authorization group enables you protect access to certain objects.
Type: Text Length: 4

Cross-Plant Product Status
The product status determines how a product is handled in different applications and in business operations (for example, purchasing, products planning, usage in bills of material ..). If a product has a product status, the system will issue either a warning or an error message (depending on which status you have chosen) if the product is used. Note: The cross-plant product status restricts the usability of the product for all plants. Please see further information in the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valid-From Date
Date from which the cross-plant product status is valid.
Type: Date

Level of Explicitness for Serial Number
Level on which the serial number must be unique.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: QM in Procurement Is Active
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. Note: This field must only be filled if there will be a related Quality Info record existing in the system. If the fields are filled and the QIR is missing, the business process will run into an error.
Type: Text Length: 1

ANP Code
For Brazil only ! Identifies the products that are controlled by the Brazilian Oil Agency.
Type: Text Length: 80

Gross Weight
Gross weight expressed in the unit of weight specified by you in the Unit of weight field. The system can use the gross weight at a later stage, depending on what kind of capacity check you run, to check storage bin capacity for warehouse management purposes.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Net Weight
Net weight expressed in the unit of weight specified by you in the Unit of weight field.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Weight (ISO Format)
Unit referring to the gross weight or net weight of the product. If a product is created with sales data, the field unit of weight gets mandatory. Please provide an entry in this field or a default unit of weight is defined in product groups settings.
Type: Text Length: 80

Length
Length of the product or its packaging, measured in the unit of dimension.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Width
Width of the product or its packaging, measured in the unit of dimension.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Height
Height of the product or its packaging, measured in the unit of dimension.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit for Length/Width/Height(ISO Format)
Unit in which the dimensions of length, breadth, and height of a product or its packaging are measured.
Type: Text Length: 80

Volume
Space that the product occupies per unit of volume. The volume refers to the unit specified in the "Volume unit" field. The volume and its unit always refer to the base unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Volume Unit
If you specify a volume, you have to enter the corresponding volume unit here.
Type: Text Length: 80

Capacity Usage
Type: Number Length: 15 Decimal: 3

Compliance Relevant
Indicates whether the product needs to be assessed for Compliance.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Product Is Configurable
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. Indicator that determines whether the product is configurable. If this indicator is set, you can assign a variant class to the product, making it possible to use it as a configurable product. Consider: if you are dealing with product type 'KMAT' the system will set this indicator automatically in the product master. This is described in detail in the migration object documentation.
Type: Text Length: 1

Order Unit of Measure (ISO Format)
Enter a value in this field only if you want to use a unit of measure differing from the base unit of measure.
Type: Text Length: 80

Purchasing Value Key
Key defining the reminder days and tolerance limits valid, as well as the shipping instructions and order acknowledgment requirement of the product for Purchasing.
Type: Text Length: 80

State of Variable Purchase Order Unit
Activation of the variable purchase order unit. The information from the field in the product master record is adopted in the purchasing info record. In the purchase order and in the source list, you can enter an order unit that differs from the one in the purchasing info record. If you wish to specify a different order unit for a requisition, you must assign a source with a different order unit to the requisition. The field also determines whether you work with conditions for each order unit.
Type: Text Length: 80

Internal Product Number
Number of firm's own (internal) inventory-managed product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Manufacturer Part Number
Specifies the number used by the manufacturer, or also by the supplier, to manage a product.
Type: Text Length: 40

Manufacturer Number
Specifies the manufacturer of the MPN product or the manufacturer's plant for which a manufacturer master record has been created.
Type: Text Length: 80

Manufacturer Part Profile
Defines how you can work with MPN products in the procurement process. If you wish to work with MPN products, enter a profile. The profile then applies to all MPN products that are assigned to this firm's own, inventory-managed product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Transportation Group
A grouping of products that share the same route and transportation requirements. The transportation group is product specific and therefore valid for all plants (where the loading group can be maintained at plant level). Transportation groups are used for automatic route scheduling during requirements.
Type: Text Length: 80

Cross-Distribution Chain Product Status
Indicates whether the product may be used in individual functions in Sales and Distribution for all distribution chains. The cross-distribution-chain product status restricts the usability of the product for all the distribution chains, that is, it defines whether a warning or error message is displayed if you include the product in a particular function.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valid From Date for Status
Date from which the cross-distribution-chain product status is valid.
Type: Date

Billing Cycle
Defines periodic billing periods and specifies the following: - The frequency of a billing period - The length of a billing period (defined by a start and end date) - The rule for determining the period end
Type: Text Length: 80

Billing Cycle Determination Rule
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Group: Packaging Materials
Groups together products that require similar packaging products.
Type: Text Length: 80

Packaging Product Type
Enter a code for a grouping of similar products, such as crates, trucks or pallets.
Type: Text Length: 80

Allowed Packaging Weight
Maximum weight of other products that are packed in this packaging product.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Allowed Packaging Weight
Unit referring to the allowed packaging weight of the product. If you specify an allowed packaging weight, you must enter the corresponding unit of weight here. Please note that the entered value needs to be mapped to a valid ISO code value after uploading the file and starting the transfer process.
Type: Text Length: 80

Excess Weight Tolerance for HU
Specifies the tolerance limit for exceeding the maximum weight allowed for a particular packaging material during packing.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 1

Allowed Packaging Volume
Maximum volume of other products that are packed in this packaging product.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Allowed Packaging Volume
Unit referring to the allowed packaging volume of the product. If you specify an allowed packaging volume, you must enter the corresponding volume unit here. Please note that the entered value needs to be mapped to a valid ISO code value after uploading the file and starting the transfer process.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Product Is Closed Packaging
For closed packaging products, during packing the total volume is not increased by the loading volume of the contents. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Excess Volume Tolerance for HU
Specifies the tolerance limit for the maximum packing volume allowed for a particular packaging product.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 1

Period Indicator for Shelf Life
Period Indicator for Shelf Life Expiration Date The period indicator specifies the unit of measurement for a period of time. Like day, week, month, year.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Conditions
Indicates what storage conditions are required for the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Temperature Conditions Indicator
Indicates the temperature conditions in which the product should be stored.
Type: Text Length: 80

Container Requirements
Key for the regulation that governs which type of container the product must be stored and shipped in.
Type: Text Length: 80

Hazardous Product Number
The hazardous product number indicates that the product is dangerous, making special precautions necessary for its storage and shipment.
Type: Text Length: 40

Label Type
Determines the way labels are created. The label type specifies, for example, whether labels are preprinted by the supplier or created on a central printer.
Type: Text Length: 80

Label Form
Determines the size and layout of the label. The label form specifies, for example, whether the label is an adhesive or a pin-on label.
Type: Text Length: 80

Number of Goods Receipt/Issue Slips
In the case of a goods receipt with reference to a purchase order or production order, the system automatically calculates the number of GR/GI slips to be printed for the product document item as a quotient from the goods receipt quantity and the quantity defined here.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Indicator: Approved Batch Rec. Required
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Total Shelf Life
Period for which the product will keep in total, that is, from the date of production to the shelf life expiration date. The period (day, week, month, year) depends on the entry in the Period indicator field.
Type: Number Length: 4

Minimum Remaining Shelf Life
Minimum amount of time for which the product must keep upon goods receipt for the goods receipt to be accepted by the system.
The period (day, week, month, year) depends on the entry in the Period indicator field.
Type: Number Length: 4

Indi. for the Shelf Life Expiration Date
Indicator for the shelf life expiration date or expiration date. In batch management, the shelf life expiration date or expiration date can be specified. You select the date type you want by using this indicator.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Percentage
Percentage specifying how much of the minimum remaining shelf life must be left if the product is to be sent to another plant (such as a store) from a central plant (such as a distribution center).
Type: Number Length: 3

Rounding Rule for Calculation of SLED
Example: Total shelf life in product master = 3 Period indicator in product master = M (month) Production date, specified at goods receipt = 10.01.2016 => SLED = 10.01.2016 + 3 months = 10.04.2016
Rounding indicator = MHD = 10.04.2016 (no rounding) Rounding indicator = '-' = 01.04.2016 (first day of month) Rounding indicator = '+' = 30.04.2016 (last day of month).
Type: Text Length: 80

Handling Indicator
Indicator that specifies how products are handled in the warehouse. The handling code could have the attribute Fragile, for example, and would therefore need to be stored in a special section of the warehouse.
Type: Text Length: 80

Warehouse Product Group
Groups products by warehousing points of view.
Type: Text Length: 80

Handling Unit Type
Specifies the HU type of the packaging product used for creating handling units.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Quality
You can use this indicator to set the quality view on client level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Sales
You can use this indicator to set the sales view on client level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Storage
You can use this indicator to set the storage view on client level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Purchasing
You can use this indicator to set the purchasing view on client level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Language Key*
The language key indicates
- the language in which texts are displayed, - the language in which you enter texts, - the language in which the system prints texts.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Description*
Text that describes the product in more detail. Note: You can maintain additional descriptions (for languages other than those provided on the 'Basic Data' sheet).
Type: Text Length: 40
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Alternative Unit of Measure (ISO Format)*
Provide only Alternative Unit of Measures (please provide the Base Unit of Measure only on sheet 'Basic Data').
Type: Text Length: 80

Denominator for Conversion to Base Unit*
Enter the number of units of the alternative unit of measure (denominator) that corresponds to the number of units of the base unit of measure (numerator). The alternative unit of measure is kilogram (kg). The base unit of measure is piece (PC). 5 kg correspond to 3 pieces. 5 kg = 3 PC => 1 kg = 3/5 PC In this case, the quotient is therefore 3/5 (the numerator being 3 and the denominator 5).
Type: Number Length: 5

Numerator for Conversion to Base Unit*
Enter the number of units of the alternative unit of measure (denominator) that corresponds to the number of units of the base unit of measure (numerator). The alternative unit of measure is kilogram (kg). The base unit of measure is piece (PC). 5 kg correspond to 3 pieces. 5 kg = 3 PC => 1 kg = 3/5 PC In this case, the quotient is therefore 3/5 (the numerator being 3 and the denominator 5).
Type: Number Length: 5

GTIN
Note: if you have additional EAN's for this unit please enter them on the Additional EAN sheet. The EAN entered here is always the Main EAN for this unit.
Type: Text Length: 18

GTIN Category
The EAN category defines the following attributes of the corresponding EAN: Type/method of number assignment (internal and/or external number assignment) and details as to whether alphanumerical EANs are allowed Check-digit algorithm that checks whether a check digit is required Prefix (for perishables EANs) Length of the EAN.
Type: Text Length: 80

Length
Length of the product or its packaging, measured in the unit of dimension for the alternative unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Width
Width of the product or its packaging, measured in the unit of dimension for the alternative unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Height
Height of the product or its packaging, measured in the unit of dimension for the alternative unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit for Length/Width/Height(ISO Format)
Unit in which the dimensions of length, width, and height of a product or its packaging for the alternative unit of measure are measured.
Type: Text Length: 80

Gross Weight
Gross weight expressed in the unit of weight specified by you in the Unit of weight field. The system can use the gross weight at a later stage, depending on what kind of capacity check you run, to check storage bin capacity for warehouse management purposes.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Weight (ISO Format)
Unit referring to the gross weight of the alternative unit of measure.
Type: Text Length: 80

Volume
Space that the product occupies per unit of volume. The volume refers to the unit specified in the "Volume unit" field. The volume and its unit always refer to the alternative unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Volume Unit (ISO Format)
If you specify a volume, you have to enter the corresponding volume unit here.
Type: Text Length: 80

Lower-Level Unit (ISO Format)
Your base unit of measure is piece. 12 pieces fit into a box, and 10 boxes fit onto a pallet. In this case, piece is the lower-level unit of measure with regard to box, and box is the lower-level unit of measure with regard to pallet. Conversely, pallet is the higher-level unit of measure with regard to box, and so on.
Type: Text Length: 80

Capacity Usage
The capacity usage of a product refers to the product quantity and is therefore specified as a variable in the system. It represents the consumption of a product in relation to the capacity.
Type: Number Length: 15 Decimal: 3
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Alternative Unit of Measure (ISO Format)*
Provide the Alternative Unit of Measures for the additional GTIN.
Type: Text Length: 80

GTIN
Note: the GTIN for the additional units is always not flagged as main GTIN. The main GTIN has to be filled on basic data sheet for the Base unit of measure and on sheet Alternative unit of measure for the alternative unit of measure. With internal number assignment, you enter the GTIN category but not a GTIN.
Type: Text Length: 18

GTIN Category
Defines how the system determines an International Article Number (EAN) to be assigned internally, and which check criteria (check digit, prefix, and so on) an EAN of this category must fulfil.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Warehouse Number*
Specifies an alphanumeric key that identifies a complex, physical warehouse within the Warehouse Management system. All activities within a warehouse, for example, goods movements and physical inventory, are assigned to a specific warehouse number. The physical warehouse where these activities take place is identified by the warehouse number.
Type: Text Length: 80

Party Entitled to Dispose*
Specifies the organization, defined as a business partner, that is entitled to dispose of stock. Usually this is the plant in which the stock is planned, and in which the ATP check is performed. For each plant, there is exactly one partner master record with plant assignment in the vendor partner role.
Type: Text Length: 80

Process Block Profile
Specifies that certain processes are not executed (process block type Error), or are only executed with a warning (process block type Warning).
Type: Text Length: 80

Process Type Determination Indicator
Specifies one of the criteria to determine a warehouse process type. It is used to group products for which you always want to use the same warehouse process type. The use of this indicator is optional.
Type: Text Length: 80

Cycle Counting Indicator
Indicates that the product is subject to the cycle counting method of the physical inventory.The indicator also defines at which time intervals a physical inventory is to be carried out for the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Required Minimum Shelf Life
Specifies the remaining shelf life of a batch during inbound delivery processing and displays a warning message if it falls short of the minimum remaining shelf life. You can use this field to specify a required minimum shelf life on the level of warehouse number and party entitled to dispose instead of specifying it on global product level.
Type: Number Length: 6 Decimal: 2

Preferred UoM
Specifies the preferred alternative unit of measure in which you usually handle a product in the warehouse. Note that you can only specify units of measures that are maintained for the corresponding product master.
Type: Text Length: 80

Putaway Control Indicator
Indicates how you want to control the putaway of certain products in certain storage types in a preferential manner.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Section Indicator
Specifies that a product is to be put away in a particular storage section.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Bin Type
Specifies the subdivision of storage bins into groups, for example, large bins and small bins.
Type: Text Length: 80

Bulk Storage Indicator
Classifies a product according to the way it is stored in a bulk storage area and how stackable it is. This classification is a decisive factor in the definition of the structure of a block within a bulk storage area.
Type: Text Length: 80

Stock Removal Control Indicator
Specifies that a product is to be removed from a particular storage type or storage type group during stock removal.
Type: Text Length: 80

Stock Determination Group
Specifies the group of products that run through the same rules during stock determination.
Type: Text Length: 80

Staging Area or Door Determination Group
Specifies the group that is used to differentiate between the requirements for a product during loading and unloading at a door or staging area.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Warehouse Number*
Specifies an alphanumeric key that identifies a complex, physical warehouse within the Warehouse Management system. All activities within a warehouse, for example, goods movements and physical inventory, are assigned to a specific warehouse number. The physical warehouse where these activities take place is identified by the warehouse number.
Type: Text Length: 80

Party Entitled to Dispose*
Specifies the organization, defined as a business partner, that is entitled to dispose of stock. Usually this is the plant in which the stock is planned, and in which the ATP check is performed. For each plant, there is exactly one partner master record with plant assignment in the vendor partner role.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Type*
Specifies a subdivision of a complex, physical warehouse. Different storage types are identified by their warehousing technique, form of organization, or their function.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Section Indicator
Specifies in which storage section the product is to be put away.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Bin Type
Specifies the subdivision of storage bins into groups, for example, large bins and small bins.
Type: Text Length: 80

Sort Rule for Empty Storage Bin Search
Specifies the prioritization of the empty bins during bin determination for putaway.
Type: Text Length: 80

Maximum Number of Bins
Specifies the maximum number of fixed storage bins allowed for the product in the storage type. Note that this value overrules the corresponding setting in the customizing for the storage type.
Type: Number Length: 3

Putaway Threshold of Storage Bin
Specifies a percentage of the storage capacity of a storage bin, below which stock is not to be added to the bin. If the indicator 'Split During Putaway' is set, this percentage is compared to the percent available capacity of the storage bin. Note that the permitted value interval lies between 0.00 and 100.00 (in percent).
A split is only performed if the available capacity is greater than the defined Threshold Addition.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Putaway Sequence
Specifies the sort sequence of storage types within a storage type group for bin determination. The bin determination searches for suitable storage bins following this sequence of storage types within a storage type group.
Type: Text Length: 2

Quantity Classification
Describes in which packaging unit a product is stored in the warehouse. It is used to determine the storage type in which the product is stored in the optimal packaging unit. It is also used to determine the operative unit of measure for the packaging specification if the quantity classification is not specified in the storage type configuration setting for the storage type.
Type: Text Length: 80

Putaway Quantity Classification
Describes in which packaging unit a product is stored in the warehouse. If included in the packaging specification level for the product, it is used during storage bin determination for putaway.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Split During Putaway
Specifies whether splitting of quantities is allowed if the entire putaway quantity does not fit in the available capacity of the destination storage bin. If this setting is selected, the Threshold Addition (in percent) is compared to the percentage of available capacity of the storage bin. A split is only performed if the available capacity is greater than the defined Threshold Addition. Depending on the customizing settings for the storage type, the system takes other settings into consideration when creating the warehouse tasks for the split, such as the maximum storage type quantity and the weight, volume, and capacity of the storage bin.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Skip During Putaway
Specifies whether the storage type is skipped during the storage bin search for putaway of the product.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: No Replenishment
Specifies that the product is not considered for replenishment for the storage type.
Type: Text Length: 1

Minimum Replenishment Quantity
Specifies the quantity that is used to calculate the replenishment quantity if the current stock falls below the defined minimum quantity. The replenishment warehouse tasks are created with a quantity rounded down to a multiple of the entered minimum replenishment quantity. Note that this does apply to replenishment for storage types for fixed bins only.
Type: Number Length: 15 Decimal: 3

Unit of Measure for Replenishm. Quantity
This unit refers to the entered minimum replenishment quantity.
Please note that the entered value needs to be mapped to a valid ISO code within the mapping tasks.
Type: Text Length: 80

Minimum Quantity
Specifies the quantity of stock below which the system triggers the creation of replenishment warehouse tasks. Note that this only applies to replenishment for storage types for fixed bins.
Type: Number Length: 15 Decimal: 3

Unit of Measure for Minimum Quantity
This unit refers to the entered minimum quantity.
Please note that the value for the unit of measure must be mapped to a valid ISO code value within the mapping tasks.
Type: Text Length: 80

Maximum Quantity
Specifies the maximum quantity that is replenished if the stock falls below the defined minimum quantity. Note that this only applies to replenishment for storage types for fixed bins.
Type: Number Length: 15 Decimal: 3

Unit of Measure for Maximum Quantity
This unit refers to the entered maximum quantity.
Please note that the value for the unit of measure must be mapped to a valid ISO code value within the mapping tasks.
Type: Text Length: 80

Min.Qty (% of Max. Qty)
Indicates the ratio of the minimum quantity to the maximum quantity.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Sales Organization*
An organizational unit responsible for the sale of certain products or services. The responsibility of a sales organization may include legal liability for products and customer claims.
Type: Text Length: 80

Distribution Channel*
The way in which products or services reach the customer. Typical examples of distribution channels are wholesale, retail, or direct sales.
Type: Text Length: 80

Sales Unit (ISO Format)
Unit of measure in which the product is sold. Enter a value in this field only if you want to use a unit of measure differing from the base unit of measure.
Type: Text Length: 80

Delivery Plant
Plant from which the goods should be delivered to the customer.
Type: Text Length: 80

Distribution-Chain-Spec. Product Status
Indicates whether, for a specific distribution chain, the product may be used in individual functions in Sales and Distribution. The distribution-chain-specific product status restricts the usability of the product for the distribution chain concerned, that is, it defines whether a warning or error message is displayed if you include the product in a particular function.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valid-From Date for Product Status
Type: Date

Indicator: Cash Discount
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Minimum Delivery Quantity
Minimum quantity that may be delivered to a customer.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Minimum Order Quantity in Base UoM
The minimum quantity a customer may order.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Delivery Unit
Only exact multiples of this number can be delivered. Example: The delivery unit consists of a number and a unit of measure. In the case of a delivery unit of 30 pieces, 30, 60, 90, and so on pieces can be delivered, but not, however, 100 pieces.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Unit of Measure (ISO) of Delivery Unit
Unit of measure in which stocks of the product are managed. The system converts all the quantities you enter in other units of measure (alternative units of measure) to the base unit of measure. You define the base unit of measure and also alternative units of measure and their conversion factors in the product master record.
Type: Text Length: 80

Default Contract Term
Type: Number Length: 3

Alternative Contract Term 1
Type: Number Length: 3

Alternative Contract Term 2
Type: Number Length: 3

Unit for Contract Term
Type: Text Length: 80

Default Extension Period
Type: Number Length: 3

Alternative Extension Period 1
Type: Number Length: 3

Alternative Extension Period 2
Type: Number Length: 3

Unit for Extension Period
Type: Text Length: 80

Rounding Profile
Key that the system uses to adjust the order proposal quantity to deliverable units.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Pricing Group
A way of grouping products to which you want to apply the same conditions. You can create condition records for a product price group using the following condition types: •Product price group (for example, all non-food products) •A combination of customer and product price group •A combination of customer price group and product price group (for example, all wholesale customers and all non-food products).
Type: Text Length: 80

Volume Rebate Group
Group definition that can be used for rebate settlement.
Type: Text Length: 80

Item Category Group*
A grouping of products that the system uses to determine item categories during the processing of sales documents.Depending on the item category group that you apply and the sales document type you are processing, the system automatically proposes an item category in the document. Example: In the standard system there are standard items, items that represent services, and items that represent packaging products.
Type: Text Length: 80

Account Assignment Group
Group of products with the same accounting requirements. Used by the system when creating an accounting document from a billing document to determine the revenue or sales deduction account. Note: this field will become mandatory for several product types foe example for product type Trading Goods, Semifinished Products, Finished Goods and also for others.
Type: Text Length: 80

Volume Commission Group
Commission group to which the product is assigned.
Type: Text Length: 80

Pricing Reference Product
Product master record that the system uses as a reference for pricing purposes. The conditions that apply to the pricing reference product also apply to the product in whose product master record the pricing reference product is stored.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Statistics Group
Specifies a statistics group for this product and helps determine which data the system updates in the logistics information system.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Group 1
You can use product groups when maintaining a product master record. Since these product groups are not used in the standard SAP System, you can use them as required, for example, for analyses.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Group 2
You can use product groups when maintaining a product master record. Since these product groups are not used in the standard SAP System, you can use them as required, for example, for analyses.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Group 3
You can use product groups when maintaining a product master record. Since these product groups are not used in the standard SAP System, you can use them as required, for example, for analyses.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Group 4
You can use product groups when maintaining a product master record. Since these product groups are not used in the standard SAP System, you can use them as required, for example, for analyses.
Type: Text Length: 80

Product Group 5
You can use product groups when maintaining a product master record. Since these product groups are not used in the standard SAP System, you can use them as required, for example, for analyses.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Country/Region*
Identifies the country/region in which the delivery originates.
Type: Text Length: 80

Tax Category 1*
Please provide the tax category and the tax classification. If you have more than 1 tax category for one country/region please use the fields Tax category 2/3/4 and so on and Tax classification 2/3/4 and so on on sheet 'Tax classification' to provide the additional tax data.
Type: Text Length: 80

Tax Classification 1*
Specifies the tax classification of a material for a particular tax category.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant*
Key uniquely identifying a plant. In Logistics a plant is an organizational unit for dividing an enterprise according to production, procurement, maintenance, and products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Type
Key that determines whether and how the product is planned. This field becomes mandatory if MRP data should be migrated. Depending on the MRP type other fields are becomming mandatory eg if you choose 'VB' for reorder-point planning you also have to specify the Reorder Point, MRP controller, Lot Sizing Procedure ...
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Controller
Specifies the number of the MRP controller or group of MRP controllers responsible for product planning for the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Availability Check
Every product that is to take part in the availability check must be assigned to a checking group. Using the checking group you can define: - Whether the products to be checked are locked during the availability check - Whether the normal ATP quantity or the cumulated ATP quantity is used.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant-Specific Product Status
The product status determines how a product is handled in different applications and in business operations (for example, purchasing, products planning, usage in bills of material ..). If a product has a product status, the system will issue either a warning or an error message (depending on which status you have chosen) if the product is used. Note: The plant-specific product status restricts the usability of the product for this specific plant. Please see further information in the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valid From Date for Status
Date from which the plant-specific product status is valid.
Type: Date

Profit Center
An organizational unit in Accounting that reflects a management-oriented structure of the organization for the purpose of internal control. Operating results for a profit center can be analyzed using either the cost of sales approach or the period accounting approach. By analyzing the fixed capital as well, you can expand your profit center for use as an investment center.
Type: Text Length: 80

Unit of Issue (ISO Format)
Enter a value in this field only if you want to use a unit of measure differing from the base unit of measure.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Batch Management Req.
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Specifies whether the product is managed in batches ('X') or not (<blank>) in the specified plant. Please consider the settings in configuration defined for 'Specify Batch Management Default by Plant'. Note: to assign the batch class to your product please use migration object 'Material classification'.
Type: Text Length: 1

Serial Number Profile
Note: Serial number management is not possible with backflushing.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Neg. Stocks Allowed in Plant
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Stock Determination Group
The stock determination group combined with the stock determination rule at plant level create a key for the stock determination strategy.
Type: Text Length: 80

Loading Group
A grouping of products that share the same loading requirements. The system uses the loading group as well as the ' Shipping conditions' and 'Delivering plant' to automatically determine the shipping point for the item in sales and transfer documents (for example Sales Order, outbound delivery).
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Co-Product
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. Indicator you can use to specify that the product can also be used as a co-product.
Type: Text Length: 1

Batch Entry in Production
This indicator refers to the components of an order, not to the finished product to be manufactured. You have the following options: •The batches can be determined when the goods issue is posted. •The batches must be entered in the order before it is released. •The batches must be entered in the order before the goods issue is posted, but not necessarily before the order is released. •The batches are determined automatically when the order is released.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Critical Part
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Documentation Required
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Inspection Interval in Days
Specifies the interval between different quality inspections.
Type: Number Length: 5

Quality Management Control Key
This key determines which conditions are valid for quality management in procurement. Note: This field must only be filled if there will be a related Quality Info record existing in the system. If the fields are filled and the QIR is missing, the business process will run into an error.
Type: Text Length: 80

Certificate Type
Defines the content of a quality certificate.
Type: Text Length: 80

Country/Region of Origin
Key of the country/region in which the product has been produced.
Type: Text Length: 80

Region of Origin
You can enter a region of the country/region from which the goods originate.
Type: Text Length: 80

Taxes in International Trade
Control code for consumption taxes in foreign trade.
Type: Text Length: 80

Material CFOP Category
Use for BRAZIL only !
The CFOP category is used to determine the proper CFOP.
Type: Text Length: 80

Purchasing Group
Internally, the purchasing group is responsible for the procurement of a product or a class of products. Externally, it is the medium through which contacts with the supplier are maintained.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Post to Inspection Stock
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Source List Required
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Autom. Purchase Oder Allowed
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Tax Indicator
Please enter the Tax indicator for product (Purchasing) for the related country/region of your plant.
Type: Text Length: 80

ABC Indicator
Indicator that classifies a product as an A, B, or C part according to its consumption value. This classification process is known as the ABC analysis. The three indicators have the following meanings: •A - important part, high consumption value •B - less important part, medium consumption value •C - relatively unimportant part, low consumption value
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Group
The MRP group contains all the products from the point of view of MRP for assigning special control parameters for the total planning run. These control parameters include, for example, the strategy group, the consumption mode, and the planning horizon.
Type: Text Length: 80

Strategy Group
The strategy group groups all the planning strategies that can be used for a particular product. The planning strategy represents the procedure used for planning a product and is (technically speaking) controlled by the MRP types.
Type: Text Length: 80

Reorder Point
For MRP Type 'VB' (Reorder-Point-Planning) you have to specify the reorder point for reorder planning. In this case this field becomes mandatory. If the stock falls below this quantity, the system flags the product for requirements planning.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Planning Time Fence
The planning fence time becomes required if you are working with MRP type which includes a firming type. This is for example the case for MRP type P1-P4 (Forecast Consumption, Auto/Manual Firming). The planning time fence specifies a period within which no automatic changes are to be made to the master plan. The planning time fence is maintained in workdays.
Type: Number Length: 3

Planning Cycle
Key that determines the day on which the product is planned and ordered.
Type: Text Length: 80

Consumption Mode
The consumption mode controls the direction on the time axis in which the system consumes requirements.
Type: Text Length: 80

Consumption Period: Backward
Determines the consumption period (in workdays) for backward consumption.
Type: Number Length: 3

Consumption Period: Forward
Determines the consumption period (in workdays) for forward consumption.
Type: Number Length: 3

Mixed MRP Indicator
Defines the indicator for planning assemblies.
Type: Text Length: 80

Planning Plant
Planning plant which specifically identifies the plant from which the planned independent requirements of the reference product comes from.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plng. Conv. Factor
Factor that the system uses to convert the quantity of the current product, measured in the base unit of measure, to the base unit quantity of the planning product.
Example:
Base unit of measure for Planning product: to Base unit of measure for Current product: kg
--> Conversion factor = 0.001 because 1 kg = 0.001 to
Type: Text Length: 10

Lot Sizing Procedure
Key that determines which lot-sizing procedure the system uses within products planning to calculate the quantity to be procured or produced.
Type: Text Length: 80

Minimum Lot Size
Minimum procurement quantity.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Maximum Lot Size
Quantity that is not allowed to be exceeded during procurement.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Fixed Lot Size
Please enter a value if you are working with Lot Sizing Procedure 'Fixed order quantity'. The fixed lot size is then a required field. Note: In this case entries in fields: Minimum Lot Size, Maximum Lot Size and Rounding value will be reset.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Storage Cost Indicator
Indicator that specifies the storage costs percentage the system uses when calculating the lot size to determine storage costs.
Type: Text Length: 80

Lot Size Independant Cost
Costs that are incurred, irrespective of lot size, for each purchase order or production order over and above the purchase order price or production costs.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Currency for Lot Size Ind. Cost
You must specify the currency for the Lot Size Independent Cost (currency of the company code belonging to the plant).
Type: Text Length: 80

Assembly Scrap (%)
Percentage scrap that occurs during production of the product if the product is an assembly. The assembly scrap is used in products planning to calculate the lot size of the assembly. The system increases the quantity to be produced by the scrap quantity calculated.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Maximum Stock Level
Quantity of the product in this plant that may not be exceeded. In products planning, the maximum stock level is used only if you have chosen Replenish to maximum stock level as the lot-sizing procedure; that is, you have entered HB as the lot size key.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Rounding Value
Value to a multiple of which the system rounds up the procurement quantity.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Takt Time
If a requirement quantity (for example, for capacity reasons) cannot be procured in a single lot, you have to schedule several receipts. In conjunction with the overlap indicator in the lot size, you can use the takt time to specify that these receipts are offset, that is, that they are delayed by the takt time. You enter the takt time in workdays.
Type: Number Length: 3

Rounding Profile
Key that the system uses to adjust the order proposal quantity to deliverable units.
Type: Text Length: 80

Safety Stock
Specifies the quantity whose purpose is to satisfy unexpectedly high demand in the coverage period.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Minimum Safety Stock
The quantity that defines the lower limit for safety stock.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Safety Time (in Workdays)
In this field, you define the number of workdays for the safety time/actual range of coverage. If you define a safety time, the requirements in MRP are brought forward on the time axis by the number of workdays you define here.
Type: Number Length: 2

Service Level Warehouse
Percentage specifying what proportion of the requirement is to be covered by the warehouse stock.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 1

Coverage Profile
The range of coverage profile contains the parameters for calculating the dynamic safety stock. This is a statistical calculation on the basis of average daily requirements.
Type: Text Length: 80

Safety Time Indicator
You use this indicator to switch on the safety time actual range of coverage for a product in MRP. If you define a safety time, the requirements in MRP are brought forward on the time axis by the number of workdays you define here. The actual range of coverage corresponds to the number of days, by which the requirements are brought forward. Using this indicator you can activate the safety time and you can set whether all requirements or only independent requirements (sales order, planned independent requirements, and so on) are to be brought forward.
Type: Text Length: 80

Safety Time Period Profile
The period profile for the safety time / actual range of coverage contains the periods (stating to/from date) with the safety time/actual range of coverage that is valid for each period.
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Relevancy for Dependent Requirements
MRP relevancy (Materials Requirements Planning) for dependent requirements: This indicator controls whether dependent requirements are relevant to MRP or not. Dependent requirements include dependent reservations and stock transfer requirements. You only use this indicator in connection with planning strategies for make-to-stock production, assembly planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

Safety Stock Method
The MRP Safety Stock Method provides different options to introduce a safety buffer.
Type: Text Length: 80

Individual / Collective Requirements
Dependent requirements indicator for individual and coll. requirements.
Type: Text Length: 80

Component Scrap in Percent
Component scrap is used in product requirements planning (MRP) to determine the quantities of components required. When exploding the bill of material (BOM), the system increases the required quantities of the components by the calculated scrap quantity.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Requirements Group
Defines whether the system groups together dependent requirements for the product on a daily basis when analyzing requirements planning. Defines whether additional individual/dependent requirements are written for components whose assembly is set such that total dependent requirements are created.
Type: Text Length: 80

Discontinuation Ind.
Identifies the product as a part to be discontinued and includes it in the discontinued parts procedure in products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

Effective-Out Date
Date from which the stocks of the product are to be used up. As soon as no more stock exists for this product, it is to be replaced by the follow-up product.
Type: Date

Follow-Up Product
Number of the product that the system uses in products planning to replace the product to be discontinued once its warehouse stock is depleted.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Repetitive Manufa. Allow
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. The repetitive manufacturing profile also determines; •how the system deals with activities when backflushing •which movement type is used to post goods receipts and goods issues •how planned order and run schedule quantities are reduced in the backflush transaction •how BOM corrections are made - if errors should occur when posting quantities to be backflushed
Type: Text Length: 1

Repetitive Manufacturing Profile
Controls, via order type, whether you are working with make-to-order repetitive manufacturing, based on sales orders, or with make-to-stock repetitive manufacturing, based on no specific orders.
Type: Text Length: 80

Procurement Type
Indicator that defines how the product is procured. The following procurements types are possible: • E = The product is produced in-house. • F = The product is procured externally. • X = The product can be both produced in-house and procured externally.
Type: Text Length: 80

Special Procurement Type
The special procurement type is used to precisely define how in-house production and external procurement is to be carried out. Example: Special Procurement for External Procurement can be Consignment, Subcontracting ...
Type: Text Length: 80

Issue Storage Location
Default storage location for produced inhouse. In the case of a product produced in-house, this is the key of the storage location that is copied to the planned order, production order, or run schedule quantity. If the product is a component, it is the issuing storage location to which a backflush is posted.
Type: Text Length: 80

Replenishment Lead Time
The total replenishment lead time is the time needed before the product is completely available again, that is, after all BOM levels have been procured or produced. It is not calculated by the system, but defined in this field as the total of the in-house production time(s) and/or the planned delivery time(s) of the longest production path. This time is necessary if, for products produced in-house, the replenishment lead time is to be taken into consideration in the availability check.
Type: Number Length: 3

Cross-Project
Determines if the system takes all stock and MRP-relevant units (inward/outward movement) in the project stock segments for all documents without account assignment into consideration.
Type: Text Length: 80

Default Storage Loc. for Ext. Procuremet
Default storage location for external procurement. Storage location that is proposed in the purchase requisition in products planning for subsequent storage of the product. In the case of a product procured externally, this storage location is proposed in the planned order in products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

Planning Calendar
Three character number (numeric or using letters) that specifically identifies a PPS-planning calendar.
Type: Text Length: 80

In-House Production Time
Specifies the time in workdays needed to produce the product in-house. In-house production time is independent of the order quantity.
Type: Number Length: 3

Planned Delivery Time
Number of calendar days needed to obtain the product or service if it is procured externally.
Type: Number Length: 3

Goods Receipt Processing Time
Number of workdays required after receiving the product for inspection and placement into storage.
Type: Number Length: 3

Backflush
Determines whether the backflush indicator is set in the production order. Here, a 3-level hierarchy logic is applied: 1. In the routing, the backflush indicator is set in the component assignment. 2. In the product master record, the backflush indicator is set to "Always Backflush". 3. In the product master record, the backflush indicator is set to "Work Center Decides", and the indicator is set in the work center.
Each product component is assigned to an operation in the production order. If a product component is backflushed, the system posts the withdrawal only upon confirmation of the operation. The withdrawal is then posted automatically.
Type: Text Length: 80

Default Supply Area
The supply area is used as an interim storage area on the shop floor which is used to provide products directly at the production line.
Type: Text Length: 80

JIT Delivery Sched.
Determines whether it is possible to generate JIT delivery schedules in addition to forecast schedules for a product specified in a purchase scheduling agreement. If you set this indicator, it will be copied into the additional data for the item when a scheduling agreement is created.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Bulk Material
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. This indicator defines a BOM item as a bulk product, which is available directly at the work center (for example, washers or grease).
Type: Text Length: 1

Scheduling Margin Key
Key that the system uses to determine the floats required for scheduling an order. You define the following floats with the scheduling margin key: • opening period • float after production (only in the case of in-house production) • float before production (only in the case of in-house production) • release period (only in the case of production order management)
Type: Text Length: 80

Period Type
Indicator specifying the periods in which the product's consumption values and forecast values are managed.
Type: Text Length: 80

Fiscal Year Variant
The fiscal year variant is used to define the fiscal year. You can define the following using a fiscal year variant: •How many posting periods are in a fiscal year •How many special periods you require •How the system determines the posting periods when posting.
Type: Text Length: 80

Splitting Indicator
Indicator that defines how the system splits forecast requirements into smaller time intervals where the MRP type is forecast-based planning and the period indicator is not "day".
Type: Text Length: 80

Reference Plant for Consumption
Plant of the reference product on whose consumption data the product forecast is based.
Type: Text Length: 80

Reference Product for Consumption
The system uses the consumption data of this product for the forecast.
Type: Text Length: 80

Use Referenced Consumption Data Until
Date until which the consumption data of the reference product is used for the product forecast.
Type: Date

Multiplier for Reference Product
Determines how much of the consumption quantity of the reference product should be used for the product forecast.
Type: Number Length: 4 Decimal: 2

Indicator: Reset Forecast Model Auto
Causes that system to reset the forecast model automatically if the tracking limit is exceeded. Furthermore, the system carries out a new model selection during the next forecast.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Correction Factors
You set this indicator if you want the correction factors to be used in the calculation of the forecast values.
Type: Text Length: 1

Base Quantity
Quantity to which the in-house production time and the processing time of the product relate.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Overdelivery Tolerance Limit
Percentage rate by which the quantity that is posted to goods receipt may exceed the production order quantity.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 1

Underdelivery Tolerance Limit
Percentage rate that specifies the percent below which the quantity posted for a goods receipt may drop below the production order quantity.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 1

Indicator: Unlimit. Overdelivery Allowed
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Production Unit (ISO Format)
Enter a value in this field only if you want to use a unit of measure differing from the base unit of measure.
Type: Text Length: 80

Production Supervisor
Group responsible for controlling the production of a product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Production Scheduling Profile
Profile that you can use to •specify that certain business transactions are carried out in parallel in a production order / process order (you can, for example, create and release an order at the same time, or release an order and print the shop papers) •trigger an automatic goods receipt •specify an overall profile for capacity leveling
Type: Text Length: 80

Setup and Teardown Time
Specifies the number of workdays needed to set up and tear down the various work centers where the product is processed. This value is the total of the setup and teardown times. The setup and teardown time is independent of the order quantity.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Interoperation Time
Specifies the number of workdays required for the transition of a produced product between work centers or work center groups.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Processing Time
Specifies the time in workdays needed to process the product in the operations at the different work centers. The processing time depends on the order quantity.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Cycle Counting Indicator
Indicates that the given product is subject to the cycle counting method of inventory.
The indicator also defines at which time intervals a physical inventory is to be carried out for the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Cycle Counting Fixed
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Set this indicator if the product is always to remain in the specified cycle counting category.
Type: Text Length: 1

Do Not Cost
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Controls whether a cost estimate or a procurement alternative can be created for a product. <empty> => Cost X => Do Not Cost
Type: Text Length: 80

Variance Key
Key that controls variance calculation. Variances can only be calculated for the orders for which a variance key has been entered. This key is defaulted through the costing view in the product master record.
Type: Text Length: 80

Costing Lot Size
Lot size of the costed object (such as a product or sales order) used in the product cost estimate.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Maximum Storage Period
The longest amount of time allowed for storing the product.
Type: Number Length: 5

Unit for Maximum Storage Period
Unit in which the maximum storage period of the product is measured.
Type: Text Length: 80

Shipping Setup Time
The time needed in shipping to set up the work centers where the product is processed. It is independent of quantity. Shipping setup time is used for capacity planning in shipping.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Shipping Processing Time
Time that is needed in shipping to process a specific quantity of a product. It refers to the base quantity for capacity planning in shipping.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Base Quantity for Capacity Planning
Quantity to which the shipping processing time refers. It is measured in the base unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Indicator: Storage
You can use this indicator to set the storage view on plant level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Work Scheduling
You can use this indicator to set the work scheduling view on plant level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Purchasing
You can use this indicator to set the purchasing view on plant level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Quality
You can use this indicator to set the quality view on plant level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Sales
You can use this indicator to set the sales view on plant level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1
