Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant*
Key uniquely identifying a plant. In Logistics a plant is an organizational unit for dividing an enterprise according to production, procurement, maintenance, and products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

Forecast Model
Indicator that defines on which forecast model the system bases its calculation of future requirements of the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Hist. Periods
The number of historical values the system uses for the forecast.
Type: Number Length: 3

Forecast Periods
Number of period splits for which a forecast should be created.
Type: Number Length: 3

Periods Per Season
Number of periods that belong to a season.You only specify a number if the forecast model is a seasonal model or if you want the system to carry out a seasonal test.
Type: Number Length: 3

Initialization Periods
Number of historical values that you want to be used for initialization.
Type: Number Length: 3

Fixed Periods
Number of periods for which the system will not recalculate the forecast values in the next forecast. The fixed periods start with the initial forecast period.
Type: Number Length: 3

Initialization
Indicator which specifies that the system should initialize a forecast model. The system also calculates the parameters that are needed for the model (such as basic value, trend value, and seasonal indices).
Type: Text Length: 80

Tracking Limit
Value that specifies the amount by which the forecast value may deviate from the actual value.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 3

Model Selection
Indicator used in forecast model selection that specifies whether the system checks the values:
T Examine for trend S Examine for seasonal fluctuations A Examine for trend and seasonal fluctuations
Type: Text Length: 80

Selection Procedure
This procedure defines how the system selects the optimum forecast model.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Param. Optimization
Indicator that causes the system to optimize the smoothing factors needed by the given forecast model.
Type: Text Length: 1

Optimization Level
By determining the optimization level, you are specifying the increment with which the system is to carry out parameter optimization. The lower the increment, the more exact but also the more time consuming the optimization process will be.
Type: Text Length: 80

Weighting Group
Key used with the "weighted moving average" forecast model to specify •how many historical values are used to calculate the forecast and •what weighting is given to each of the historical values
Type: Text Length: 80

Alpha Factor
The alpha factor is the smoothing factor for the basic value. The system uses this factor in the forecast to update the basic value.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 2

Beta Factor
The beta factor is the smoothing factor for the trend value. The system uses this factor to update the trend value in forecasting.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 2

Gamma Factor
The gamma factor is the smoothing factor for the seasonal index. The system uses this factor to update the seasonal index.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 2

Delta Factor
The delta factor is the smoothing factor for the mean absolute deviation (MAD). The system uses this factor to update the mean absolute deviation in forecasting.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 2
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant*
Key uniquely identifying a plant. In Logistics a plant is an organizational unit for dividing an enterprise according to production, procurement, maintenance, and products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Location*
Number of the storage location in which the product is stored. A plant may contain one or more storage locations.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Bin
Specifies the smallest addressable unit in a warehouse that is sometimes referred to as a slot. A storage bin identifies the exact location in the warehouse where goods can be stored. Note that the storage bin must already exist.
Type: Text Length: 10
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant*
Key uniquely identifying a plant. In Logistics a plant is an organizational unit for dividing an enterprise according to production, procurement, maintenance, and products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

PRT Usage
With this key you control in which task lists the production resources/tools may be used.
Type: Text Length: 80

Grouping Key 1
An open key, with which you can group together production resources/tools (for example, measurement instruments or test equipment).
Type: Text Length: 80

Grouping Key 2
An open key, with which you can group together production resources/tools (for example, measurement instruments or test equipment).
Type: Text Length: 80

PRT Control Profile
Key with which you can specify how production resources/tools should be handled in the task list or in the order.
Production resources/tools can be processed in the following ways: •scheduling •confirming •costing •printing
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: PRT Control Profile Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Standard Text Key
Key with which you allocate a text (standard text) to a production resource/tool. This text can contain different specifications, for example, information about using the production resource or tool.
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: Standard Text Key Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Quantity Formula
Formula for Calculating the Total Quantity of PRT (production resources/tools)
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: Quantity Formula Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Usage Value Formula
Formula for Calculating the Total Usage Value of PRT
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: Usage Value Formula Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Start Date Reference
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: Start Refer. of Usage Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Offset to Start
Offset between the reference date and the start of production resource/tool (PRT) usage. The offset and the reference date are needed to calculate the start date for PRT usage.
Type: Number Length: 5

Offset to Start Unit (ISO Format)
Unit of time in which the offset to the reference date is expressed.
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: Offset Start of Usage Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

End Date Reference
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: End Refer. of Usage Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Offset to End
Offset between the reference date and the finish of the production resource/tool usage. The offset and the reference date are needed to calculate the finish date of production resource usage.
Type: Number Length: 5

Offset to End Unit (ISO Format)
Unit of time in which the offset to the reference date is expressed.
Type: Text Length: 80

Ind: Offset End of Usage Is Referenced
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Indicator: Preferred Inspection Type
If you set this indicator, the system creates preferred inspection lots with this inspection type for the corresponding inspection lot origins. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Active
If you set this indicator, inspection lots can be processed for this product and inspection type. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Post to Inspection Stock
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Serial Numbers Possible
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Dynamic Modification Rule
Contains the definition of inspection stages and the conditions that lead to changes in inspection stages.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Skips Allowed
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, skips are allowed for inspection characteristics during sample calculation. If you do not set this indicator, the system chooses the next inspection stage in the dynamic modification rule that is not a skip, instead of the skip defined in the quality level during sample calculation.
Type: Text Length: 1

Inspection Lot Summary
This field is only relevant for inspections of warehouse-managed stock. You can group goods that need to be inspected into one single inspection lot. For example, you can group delivered products by purchase order so that even if the products are delivered in multiple deliveries, the products are grouped into one inspection lot.
Type: Text Length: 80

Control Lot Creation
Control of Inspection Lot Creation (Lot Summary)
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Inspection with Task List
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, the inspection is performed based on the task list
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Inspect by Batch
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Ind: Automatic Specification Assignment
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If this indicator is set, the system trys to assign a task list or a material specification automatically.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Inspect Characteristics
If you set this indicator, characteristic-based results recording is performed for the inspection lot.
Type: Text Length: 1

Sampling Procedure
Sampling Procedure by which the sample size for an inspection is determined.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: 100% Inspection
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, the system treats the inspection lot size as the sample size. Dependencies: If you define a 100% inspection for the product, you must manually valuate the inspection characteristics.
Type: Text Length: 1

Inspection Percentage
Inspection Percentage This field is only relevant, when you carry out an inspection without reference to a task list or material specification. In this case, the total sample size is calculated on the basis of the percentage rate taken from the lot size.
Type: Number Length: 3

Indicator: Manual Sample Calculation
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, you must trigger the sample calculation manually in the inspection lot.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Manual Sample Entry
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If you set this indicator, you must manually enter the sample size for the inspection lot.
Type: Text Length: 1

Average Inspection Days
Average duration of an inspection (in days) for each inspection type.
Type: Number Length: 3

Indicator: Automatic Usage Decision
If you set this indicator, the system can automatically make usage decisions for inspection lots. The inspection lots must meet the following conditions: All characteristics have been completed. None of the characteristics have been rejected. No defects have been recorded, or quality notifications created. The waiting time must have elapsed.
Type: Text Length: 1

Quality Score Procedure
The procedure used to calculate the quality score for the inspection lot.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Area*
Number of the MRP area for which product requirements planning should be carried out separately.
Type: Text Length: 80

Plant*
Key uniquely identifying a plant. In Logistics a plant is an organizational unit for dividing an enterprise according to production, procurement, maintenance, and products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Type
Key that determines whether and how the product is planned. This field becomes mandatory if MRP data should be migrated. Depending on the MRP type other fields are becomming mandatory eg if you choose 'VB' for reorder-point planning you also have to specify the Reorder Point, MRP controller, Lot Sizing Procedure ...
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Controller
Specifies the number of the MRP controller or group of MRP controllers responsible for product planning for the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Group
The MRP group contains all the products from the point of view of MRP for assigning special control parameters for the total planning run. These control parameters include, for example, the strategy group, the consumption mode, and the planning horizon.
Type: Text Length: 80

Reorder Point
For MRP Type 'VB' (Reorder-Point-Planning) you have to specify the reorder point for reorder planning. In this case this field becomes mandatory. If the stock falls below this quantity, the system flags the product for requirements planning.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Planning Time Fence
The planning fence time becomes required if you are working with MRP type which includes a firming type. This is for example the case for MRP type P1-P4 (Forecast Consumption, Auto/Manual Firming). The planning time fence specifies a period within which no automatic changes are to be made to the master plan. The planning time fence is maintained in workdays.
Type: Number Length: 3

Planning Cycle
Key that determines the day on which the product is planned and ordered.
Type: Text Length: 80

Lot Sizing Procedure
Key that determines which lot-sizing procedure the system uses within products planning to calculate the quantity to be procured or produced.
Type: Text Length: 80

Minimum Lot Size
Minimum procurement quantity.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Maximum Lot Size
Quantity that is not allowed to be exceeded during procurement.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Fixed Lot Size
Please enter a value if you are working with Lot Sizing Procedure 'Fixed order quantity'. The fixed lot size is then a required field. Note: In this case entries in fields: Minimum Lot Size, Maximum Lot Size and Rounding value will be reset.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Storage Cost Indicator
Indicator that specifies the storage costs percentage the system uses when calculating the lot size to determine storage costs.
Type: Text Length: 80

Lot Size Independent Cost
Costs that are incurred, irrespective of lot size, for each purchase order or production order over and above the purchase order price or production costs.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Currency for Lot Size Ind. Cost
You must specify the currency for the Lot Size Independent Cost (currency of the company code belonging to the plant).
Type: Text Length: 80

Assembly Scrap (%)
Percentage scrap that occurs during production of the product if the product is an assembly. The assembly scrap is used in products planning to calculate the lot size of the assembly. The system increases the quantity to be produced by the scrap quantity calculated.
Type: Number Length: 5 Decimal: 2

Maximum Stock Level
Quantity of the product in this plant that may not be exceeded. In products planning, the maximum stock level is used only if you have chosen Replenish to maximum stock level as the lot-sizing procedure; that is, you have entered HB as the lot size key.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Rounding Value
Value to a multiple of which the system rounds up the procurement quantity.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Takt Time
If a requirement quantity (for example, for capacity reasons) cannot be procured in a single lot, you have to schedule several receipts. In conjunction with the overlap indicator in the lot size, you can use the takt time to specify that these receipts are offset, that is, that they are delayed by the takt time. You enter the takt time in workdays.
Type: Number Length: 3

Rounding Profile
Key that the system uses to adjust the order proposal quantity to deliverable units.
Type: Text Length: 80

Safety Stock
Specifies the quantity whose purpose is to satisfy unexpectedly high demand in the coverage period.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Service Level Warehouse
Percentage specifying what proportion of the requirement is to be covered by the warehouse stock.
Type: Number Length: 3 Decimal: 1

Coverage Profile
The range of coverage profile contains the parameters for calculating the dynamic safety stock. This is a statistical calculation on the basis of average daily requirements.
Type: Text Length: 80

Safety Time Indicator
You use this indicator to switch on the safety time actual range of coverage for a product in MRP. If you define a safety time, the requirements in MRP are brought forward on the time axis by the number of workdays you define here. The actual range of coverage corresponds to the number of days, by which the requirements are brought forward. Using this indicator you can activate the safety time and you can set whether all requirements or only independent requirements (sales order, planned independent requirements, and so on) are to be brought forward.
Type: Text Length: 80

Safety Time (in Workdays)
In this field, you define the number of workdays for the safety time/actual range of coverage. If you define a safety time, the requirements in MRP are brought forward on the time axis by the number of workdays you define here.
Type: Number Length: 2

Safety Time Period Profile
The period profile for the safety time / actual range of coverage contains the periods (stating to/from date) with the safety time/actual range of coverage that is valid for each period.
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Relevancy for Dep. Requirements
MRP relevancy (Materials Requirements Planning) for dependent requirements: This indicator controls whether dependent requirements are relevant to MRP or not. Dependent requirements include dependent reservations and stock transfer requirements. You only use this indicator in connection with planning strategies for make-to-stock production, assembly planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

MRP Safety Stock Method
The MRP Safety Stock Method provides different options to introduce a safety buffer.
Type: Text Length: 80

Special Procurement Type
The special procurement type is used to precisely define how in-house production and external procurement is to be carried out. Example: Special Procurement for External Procurement can be Consignment, Subcontracting ...
Type: Text Length: 80

Default Storage Loc. for Ext. Proc.
Default storage location for external procurement. Storage location that is proposed in the purchase requisition in products planning for subsequent storage of the product. In the case of a product procured externally, this storage location is proposed in the planned order in products planning.
Type: Text Length: 80

Planning Calendar
Three character number (numeric or using letters) that specifically identifies a PPS-planning calendar.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Consider Planned Deliv. Time
This is an indicator field. If you use Planned Delivery Time of the MRP Area, enter X. If you use Planned Delivery Time of the Plant, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Planned Delivery Time
Number of calendar days needed to obtain the product or service if it is procured externally.
Type: Number Length: 3

Issue Storage Location
Default storage location for produced inhouse. In the case of a product produced in-house, this is the key of the storage location that is copied to the planned order, production order, or run schedule quantity. If the product is a component, it is the issuing storage location to which a backflush is posted.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Area*
Valuation area is the organizational level at which the product is valuated.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Type
This is a further subdivision of the valuation category. The valuation category defines the valuation types allowed for a product. If a product is valuated by origin (for example, valuation category H), you can define the possible countries of origin as valuation types. For valuation category 'Single batch Valuation' you have to provide for each batch an own valuation type with the detailed price information. Note: you have to define one valuation header record with specified valuation category and initial valuation type and one dependent record for each valuation type. Further information can be found in the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 10

Valuation Category
Determines whether stocks of the material are valuated together or separately. In the case of split valuation, this indicator also determines which valuation types are allowed, that is, the criteria by which stocks can be valuated. Further information can be found the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 80

Price Control Determination
The Price determination indicator determines whether price determination is: •Transaction-based •Single-level / multilevel
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Class*
Assignment of a product to a group of G/L accounts
With other factors, the valuation class determines the G/L accounts that are updated as a result of a valuation-relevant transaction or event such as a goods movement. The valuation class makes it possible to: •Post the stock values of products of the same product type to different G/L accounts •Post the stock values of products of different product types to the same G/L account
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Class for Sales Order
Default value for the valuation class for valuated sales order stocks of the product. You need specify a valuation class here only if you want to assign a different valuation class to this type of stock than that assigned to other valuated stocks of the product. This makes it possible to define other allowed G/L accounts for these stocks.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Class for Project Stock
Default value for the valuation class for valuated project stocks of the product. You need specify a valuation class here only if you want to assign a different valuation class to this type of stock than that assigned to other valuated stocks of the product. This makes it possible to define other allowed G/L accounts for these stocks.
Type: Text Length: 80

Price Control*
Indicates the price control used to valuate the stock of a product. You have the following options: • S - Standard price • V - Moving average price
Type: Text Length: 80

Currency*
You must specify the company code currency belonging to the valuation area.
Type: Text Length: 80

Inventory Price Moving Average
Moving average price if the product has price control indicator 'V' for moving average price. The system calculates the moving average price automatically by dividing the product value in the stock account by the total of all storage location stocks in the plant concerned. It changes the price with each valuation-relevant movement.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Inventory Price Standard Price
Standard price of the product if the product has price control indicator 'S' for standard price. The valuation of product stocks at standard prices means that all goods movements are valuated at the same price over an extended period.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5

Future Price
Price at which the product will be valuated as from a precise date in the future. The future price is valid as of the date you enter in the relevant field.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Date of Which Future Price Is Valid From
Type: Date

Planned Price 1
Price that the system can take as a basis for product costing.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Planned Price Date 1
Date as from which planned price 1 is valid for the product.
Type: Date

Planned Price 2
Price that the system can take as a basis for product costing.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Planned Price Date 2
Date as from which planned price 2 is valid for the product.
Type: Date

Planned Price 3
Price that the system can take as a basis for product costing.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Planned Price Date 3
Date as from which planned price 3 is valid for the product.
Type: Date

Tax Price 1
Price at which the product is valuated for tax purposes.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Tax Price 2
Price at which the product is valuated for tax purposes, and which is derived in the determination of lowest value. It relates to the price unit of the previous year.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Tax Price 3
Price at which the product is valuated for reasons of tax law.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5

Commercial Price 1
Price at which the product is valuated for commercial purposes.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Commercial Price 2
Price at which the product is valuated from the commercial viewpoint, and which is derived in the determination of lowest value.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Commercial Price 3
Price at which the product is valuated for reasons of commercial law. This price is given by the FIFO valuation.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

LIFO/FIFO Relevant
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 80

Devaluation Indicator
A devaluation indicator representing the number of years a product has already been classed as "slow or non-moving".
Type: Number Length: 2

Usage of the Product
Use for BRAZIL only !
This indicator shows the intended usage of a particular product in inventory. Please note that each usage of a product must have a separate valuation. As soon as a product has more than one usage in a valuation area then valuation types can be used.
Type: Text Length: 80

Origin of the Product
Use for BRAZIL only !
This indicator shows whether the products were produced inside Brazil or were imported. If the goods were imported, it also indicates whether they were imported directly by the company or not.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Produced In-House
Use for BRAZIL only !
If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. This indicator shows whether the product was produced by the company or another one. It is used for purpose of CFOP determination. If the indicator is set the product was produced by the company. If the indicator is not set the product was produced by another company.
Type: Text Length: 1

Product-Related Origin
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If this indicator is set, the product number will be written to the cost element itemization in the Controlling module.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: With Qty Structure
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Origin Group
Used for subdividing material and overhead costs.
When material costs are calculated, each material is given a general ledger account and thus is assigned to a primary cost element. If you enter an origin group for the material, the combination of source group and cost element is updated to the CO component. This means that you can Define calculation bases for determination of overhead rates for particular material groups. Define cost components in product costing for particular material groups. Create rules for activating work in process for each material group.
When overhead is calculated, an object is debited with overhead costs and another object (usually a cost center) is credited. For more information on the origin of overhead rates in the credit record, specify an origin group in the credit key. The system then displays the origin group in Cost Center Accounting, as well as the overhead cost element.
Type: Text Length: 80

Overhead Group
Key that groups products to which the same overhead is applied.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Accounting
You can use this indicator to set the accounting view on valuation level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Costing
You can use this indicator to set the costing view on valuation level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 - Adjusted Scope - 07.13.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Area*
Valuation area is the organizational level at which the product is valuated.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Type
This is a further subdivision of the valuation category. The valuation category defines the valuation types allowed for a product. If a product is valuated by origin (for example, valuation category H), you can define the possible countries of origin as valuation types. For valuation category 'Single batch Valuation' you have to provide for each batch an own valuation type with the detailed price information. Note: you have to define one valuation header record with specified valuation category and initial valuation type and one dependent record for each valuation type. Further information can be found in the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 10

Valuation Category
Determines whether stocks of the material are valuated together or separately. In the case of split valuation, this indicator also determines which valuation types are allowed, that is, the criteria by which stocks can be valuated. Further information can be found the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 80

Currency Type*
The currency type specifies the role of a currency in the system. In addition to the local currency (company code currency), you can use parallel currencies per company code.
Type: Text Length: 80

Price Control*
Indicates the price control used to valuate the stock of a product. You have the following options: • S - Standard price • V - Moving average price
Type: Text Length: 80

Currency*
You must specify the currency belonging to the provided currency type.
Type: Text Length: 80

Inventory Price Moving Average
Moving average price if the product has price control indicator 'V' for moving average price. The system calculates the moving average price automatically by dividing the product value in the stock account by the total of all storage location stocks in the plant concerned. It changes the price with each valuation-relevant movement.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Inventory Price Standard Price
Standard price of the product if the product has price control indicator 'S' for standard price. The valuation of product stocks at standard prices means that all goods movements are valuated at the same price over an extended period.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5

Future Price*
Price at which the product will be valuated as from a precise date in the future. The future price is valid as of the date you enter in the relevant field.
Type: Number Length: 14 Decimal: 3 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Future Price From*
Date from which a Future Price is to be valid.
Type: Date

Price Unit for Future Price
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5
